title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Query Repair Bay
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a guarded ticket-mutation workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Insert Values Select
steps.instruction:
debug case — Import ticket 202 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence. Competency probe: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.

Inspect the Insert Values Select trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Mutation Returning Audit
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-mutation-returning-audit-1xanan" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; bounded mutation and changed-row evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations.
